details.notes: All 11 assets embedded as data:image/png;base64 URIs. window.open() used only in CTA handler via window.__openStore(). MRAID mraid.open() used as primary store opener when MRAID available.

All 6 event handlers registered on canvas with {passive:false}. preventDefault() called in every handler. Game starts only on user tap/click in 'title' state (onDown -> startLevel(0)). Game cannot auto-advance levels.

CTA overlay (#cta-overlay) shown via showCTA() on: fail, win-all-levels, and timer expiry (35s). Button has gradient background, box-shadow, CSS ctaIn keyframe entrance animation (cubic-bezier elastic), and requestAnimationFrame-based pulse scale loop.

details.setIntervalNotes: setInterval used only for ambient lava drip effect (non-loop), which is acceptable
details.notes: DPR handled: canvas.width=W*DPR, canvas.height=H*DPR, ctx.scale(DPR,DPR). Canvas style width/height set to CSS pixel values. Game loop is pure requestAnimationFrame.

9 sounds implemented: sawtooth sweep (pinPull), noise buffer+lowpass (lavaFlow), noise+square oscillator blend (enemyDeath), sine arpeggio (heroSave, levelClear), sine ding (coinCollect), square click (buttonClick), sine rumble (shake), sawtooth descend (fail). AudioContext lazy-init on first user gesture.

5 easing functions defined in Ease object. tween() function accepts object/property/from/to/duration/ease/callback. Used throughout: pin drag-back (easeOutBack), hero jump (easeOutBack/easeOutBounce), star reveal (easeOutElastic), pin removal alpha (linear), hero hit (linear).

POOL=200 particles pre-allocated at startup via for loop pushing {active:false,...} objects. spawnParticle() reuses inactive slots. updateParticles() iterates pool and updates only active particles. No new object creation per frame.
triggerShake(intensity, duration) with linear decay: var decay=1-(shakeEl/shakeDur); shakeX/Y=(Math.random()*2-1)*intensity*decay. ctx.translate(shakeX,shakeY) applied per frame.

mraid.isReady() checked before addEventListener('ready'). mraid.open(url) used in CTA handler. viewableChange listener registered but callback body is empty - does not auto-resume game on viewability change. mraid.getState() checked for 'hidden' state. MRAID 2.0 integration is functionally complete for store open; viewableChange hook is cosmetic no-op.

Level 1: Tutorial, 1 pin, guaranteed win, animated hand hint. Level 2: 2 pins, order puzzle (pin2 must be first), retry allowed. Level 3: 3 pins, complex routing, fail_bait=true, boss enemy, fail message 'YOU NEED MORE POWER!'.

State machine: loading (asset load) -> title (TAP TO START) -> intro (0.95s level intro overlay) -> playing (interactive) -> result (1.5s with stars/coins) -> CTA. onWin/onFail transitions implemented. Global 35s timer triggers CTA. All transitions via gameState string variable.

warnings: Asset key 'wall_tex' does not match spec name 'wall' (minor naming inconsistency)
Asset 'wall_tex' is defined in ASSETS but walls are rendered procedurally (canvas shapes) - wall asset not used in drawWalls()
Asset 'button' is defined in ASSETS but not referenced in any drawImage() call - may be unused
assets.notes: All 11 assets present as Base64 inline data URIs. Functional impact: nil - walls use procedural canvas drawing with fallback (no broken images). button asset unused but loaded.

feedbacks.description: MRAID viewableChange handler registered but body is empty (no-op). On viewability resume, game does not auto-trigger any action.
feedbacks.suggestion: Consider adding resumption logic: if(v && gameState==='title') { /* auto-start or play attract animation */ }. Low priority for ad network compliance.

feedbacks.description: setInterval used for ambient lava drip effect (lavaDripInt). This is not the main game loop (which correctly uses requestAnimationFrame), but setInterval for effects can drift.
feedbacks.suggestion: Consider replacing lavaDripInt setInterval with a timer variable checked inside the requestAnimationFrame loop for more precise timing control.

feedbacks.description: Asset key 'wall_tex' does not match the spec name 'wall'. The asset is loaded but walls are drawn procedurally, so the loaded image is never rendered.
feedbacks.suggestion: Either rename the asset key to 'wall' for consistency with the spec, or use the wall texture in drawWalls() for visual quality. Alternatively, remove the asset to reduce file size (minor - Base64 PNG is small).

feedbacks.description: Asset 'button' (Button.png) is loaded as Base64 but never referenced in any drawImage() call. The button asset is unused.
feedbacks.suggestion: Use ASSETS.button.img for the UI buttons (result screen, CTA area) or remove from ASSETS to save load time and reduce file size.

feedbacks.description: MAX_TIME is set to 35 seconds but game_design.yaml specifies session_length of 25s. Timer mismatch may cause CTA to appear later than intended.
feedbacks.suggestion: Align MAX_TIME with the 25s session target: var MAX_TIME=25; This ensures the timer-based CTA trigger fires at the specified design deadline.

highlights: File size 309KB - well within all network limits (84.8% headroom vs Facebook 2MB limit)
All 4 standard Playable validation stages passed
5 easing functions in custom tween engine
200-slot object pool particle system with pre-allocation
Screen shake with intensity/duration/decay across 3 presets
MRAID 2.0 integration: isReady check, ready listener, viewableChange, mraid.open()
3-level progression: Tutorial -> Order Puzzle -> Fail Bait Boss
All 11 required assets embedded as Base64 inline
